title: Tonys Comeback

jokes.text: Jim, we've been your friends for three years. / Oh, then it's high time we shook hands.
jokes.setup: Alex pointing out they've been friends for three years
jokes.punchline: Jim treating it as if they're meeting for the first time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim Ignatowski character moment - his spacey, disconnected personality perfectly executed with great economy

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: We've been friends for three years.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Three years? Well, it's nice to meet you.

jokes.text: Oh, and they gave you a ring for that? How cute.
jokes.setup: Lucius mentions his Super Bowl ring
jokes.punchline: Elaine dismissively calling a Super Bowl ring 'cute'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Elaine's tendency to downplay others' achievements

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: That's cute.

jokes.text: I was wondering why I never heard of you.
jokes.setup: Lucius admits he was third string
jokes.punchline: Elaine's blunt observation about his obscurity
jokes.explanation: Classic Elaine tactlessness - direct and uncomfortable but true

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Third string? I didn't even know you were in the phone book.

jokes.text: How come you guys always slap each other on the butt?
jokes.setup: The guys want to ask football questions
jokes.punchline: Elaine's inappropriately personal first question
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor with Elaine's characteristic lack of filter

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: So, um, tell me... do you ever think about me when you're with your wife?

jokes.text: They were trying to save his life.
jokes.setup: Tony calling the boxing commission's decision frivolous
jokes.punchline: Alex's dry correction about their actual motivation
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery that reveals the truth about Tony's boxing career

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: That decision was frivolous!

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: No, Tony. They weren't being frivolous. They were being bribed.

jokes.text: You mean, you'd work out with a washed-up has-been? / No, but I'd work out with you if you'd stop talking like that.
jokes.setup: Tony's self-deprecating question
jokes.punchline: Lucius's clever reversal refusing the premise
jokes.explanation: Expertly constructed misdirection with a positive message - Lucius refuses Tony's self-pity

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Why would someone like you want to be friends with someone like me?

dialogue.speaker: Lucius
dialogue.line: I don't know. Why would I?

jokes.text: Oh, I can't wait to see this.
jokes.setup: Louie announces he'll make an example of the new driver
jokes.punchline: Alex's anticipation of Louie getting his comeuppance
jokes.explanation: Simple anticipation joke setting up the upcoming confrontation

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Finally, someone's gonna teach Louie a lesson.

jokes.text: But don't ever touch me.
jokes.setup: Lucius giving a respectful speech about following rules
jokes.punchline: The firm boundary he sets with Louie
jokes.explanation: Perfect character establishment - respectful but firm, shutting down Louie's physical intimidation

jokes.text: Where were you when I was still fighting? / Why? Weren't you in good shape then? / Oh, yeah. I was rock solid. You should have heard the thud when I hit the canvas.
jokes.setup: Tony lamenting not having Lucius as a trainer before
jokes.punchline: Tony's self-aware joke about his boxing failures
jokes.explanation: Great self-deprecating humor that acknowledges Tony's poor boxing record with a vivid image

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: You know, if I had you as a trainer when I was coming up, things would've been different.

dialogue.speaker: Lucius
dialogue.line: How so?

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: I'd probably be broke in a different neighborhood.

jokes.text: I don't know what you're getting at here, but I swear, I'm not the least bit attracted to you.
jokes.setup: Lucius mentions their 'symbiotic relationship'
jokes.punchline: Tony's misunderstanding of the word
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Tony's misunderstanding of big words

dialogue.speaker: Lucius
dialogue.line: We have a symbiotic relationship.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Symbiotic? What's that mean?

dialogue.speaker: Lucius
dialogue.line: It means we both benefit from knowing each other.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Oh, you mean like... parasitic?

jokes.text: Oh. Phew!
jokes.setup: Lucius explains what symbiotic means
jokes.punchline: Tony's visible relief reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat completing the misunderstanding joke

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: (visible relief reaction)

jokes.text: I'm not laughing.
jokes.setup: Lucius worried people would laugh at his duck story
jokes.punchline: Tony's literal, earnest response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tony's sincerity and literalness

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: So what? Let 'em laugh. At least they're listening to you.

jokes.text: I'm still not laughing.
jokes.setup: Continuation of the duck story
jokes.punchline: Tony's repeated earnest assurance
jokes.explanation: Simple callback extending the previous joke

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: The duck, he's gonna be fine. I'm telling you, the duck is gonna be fine. You gotta have faith in the duck. The duck knows what he's doing. Trust me on this—the duck is gonna be fine.

jokes.text: Do it for the duck.
jokes.setup: The inspirational duck story
jokes.punchline: Lucius applying the lesson to Tony's sit-ups
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd motivational phrase that becomes quotable and meaningful within the show's universe

jokes.text: I'm talking to a fish.
jokes.setup: Phone call from the Miami Dolphins
jokes.punchline: Jim's literal interpretation of the team name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim moment - his disconnected logic applied to a team name

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Hello? Yes, this is Jim Ignatowski. The Miami Dolphins? You're calling about the football team? No, no, I'm sorry, I can't help you. I don't know anything about dolphins. I mean, I know they're mammals, they're very intelligent, but I've never actually met one. And I certainly don't know any that play football.

jokes.text: Excuse me. I-I mean mammal.
jokes.setup: Jim correcting himself about dolphins
jokes.punchline: His attempt at scientific accuracy while still being wrong
jokes.explanation: Good extension showing Jim's pseudo-intellectual corrections

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Actually, dolphins aren't fish, they're mammals.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: They breathe air through a blowhole.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Well, technically it's not a hole, it's more of a... modified nostril.

jokes.text: Boy, I knew you guys were smart.
jokes.setup: Continuing the dolphin conversation
jokes.punchline: Jim's amazement at dolphin intelligence in context
jokes.explanation: Continues Jim's misunderstanding with characteristic wonder

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: You know, dolphins are so intelligent they can communicate with each other underwater. They use echolocation and everything.

jokes.text: They can dial!
jokes.setup: Jim's amazement at dolphin intelligence
jokes.punchline: His wonder at their telephone abilities
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Jim's misunderstanding - his awe at dolphin dexterity

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Dolphins are so intelligent. They can communicate with each other underwater, they solve complex problems, they even use tools.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: But you know what really amazes me? They've never had to pay a phone bill.

jokes.text: Know him? I once broke his ribs.
jokes.setup: Asked if he knows Bob Griese
jokes.punchline: The violent nature of their 'relationship'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about football violence disguised as casual friendship

jokes.text: Ah, the ties that bind.
jokes.setup: Lucius mentioning breaking Bob Griese's ribs
jokes.punchline: Alex's dry comment on this 'friendship'
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to the violence-as-bonding concept

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: That's quite a friendship you've got there.

jokes.text: Hey, I ain't spoiling this party. No matter what they tell me, I'm telling you that I got my license back.
jokes.setup: Tony calling the boxing commission
jokes.punchline: His plan to lie about bad news
jokes.explanation: Sets up expectation that he'll lie, making the truth more surprising

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Hello, is this the boxing commission? Yeah, hi, I'm calling about my fighter. No, no, he's fine, he's fine. Look, I got some bad news about the fight tomorrow night. Yeah, well, the thing is, he can't fight. No, he's not injured or anything. It's just that... he's dead. No, no, I'm not joking. He died this afternoon. Yeah, well, here's what I'm thinking. Tomorrow night, I show up with another guy, same weight, same reach, I tell everybody it's him, he just got a real good tan.

jokes.text: I really did get my license back! / Yeah, we know, Tony. / I mean it! / Yeah. / I know what I said, but I mean... / It's okay, Tony. / I really got my license back. / Yeah, sure, Tony.
jokes.setup: Tony's earlier declaration he'd lie
jokes.punchline: Nobody believes him when he tells the truth
jokes.explanation: Great ironic payoff where his honesty is undermined by his earlier plan to lie

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Nobody believes me when I tell the truth.

jokes.text: Hey, if I'm lying, you could punch me right in the face. / He got his license back.
jokes.setup: Tony's desperate attempt to prove he's telling the truth
jokes.punchline: Alex immediately believing him based on the offer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logic - Alex knows Tony wouldn't offer to be punched if he was lying about boxing

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: I'm telling you, Alex, it's the truth! I swear on my mother's life!

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: You'd do that for a five-dollar bill.

jokes.text: Say hello to Flipper.
jokes.setup: Lucius going to play for the Dolphins
jokes.punchline: Elaine's TV reference
jokes.explanation: Weak pop culture reference that doesn't add much beyond the dolphin connection

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: The Dolphins? That's great! You know, I loved that show.

jokes.text: Why would you want to be Miss America? / To help mankind throughout the world and to uphold the ideals of the Miss America pageant! / You yutz!
jokes.setup: Alex's confusion about Elaine's dream
jokes.punchline: Elaine's pitch-perfect pageant answer and Louie's dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Elaine's earnestness and pageant knowledge

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: I was in a beauty pageant, and they asked me what I wanted to do with my life, and I said 'I want to be a flight attendant and see the world.'

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: That's the dumbest thing I ever heard.

jokes.text: One minute I wanted to work for world peace. The next minute I wanted to stamp out poverty. The next minute I wanted to eat a chocolate chip cookie. This big.
jokes.setup: Jim describing his changing dreams
jokes.punchline: The bathos from world peace to a giant cookie
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke with perfect Jim absurdity - from noble causes to basic desires

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I used to dream about world peace, ending hunger, curing disease. Now I dream about a giant cookie.

jokes.text: Getting caught up in Family Feud is not a great reason.
jokes.setup: Tony skipping workouts
jokes.punchline: The trivial reason being game shows
jokes.explanation: Relatable humor about being distracted by TV

dialogue.speaker: Duke
dialogue.line: Hey Tony, why you been skipping all your workouts at the gym?

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: I've been busy, Duke. I got a good reason.

dialogue.speaker: Duke
dialogue.line: Yeah? What's the reason?

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Game shows.

jokes.text: I could help you do that.
jokes.setup: Tony's list of lazy activities
jokes.punchline: Alex offering to help with the bad habits
jokes.explanation: Good dry humor from Alex about Tony's lack of motivation

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: You know, I've been thinking about all my lazy habits. I smoke too much, I drink too much, I don't exercise.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Well, Tony, I'd be happy to help you break those bad habits.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Yeah? How?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I could hold your cigarettes while you're not looking, hide your bottle, and tie you to the back of my cab while I drive around the city.

jokes.text: Not a cheerleader? That's putting it lightly.
jokes.setup: Alex's brutal honesty about boxing
jokes.punchline: Tony's observation about Alex's negativity
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Alex's claim about not being a cheerleader

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: You know, Alex, you're like a boxer who's always looking at the canvas instead of your opponent. You're so busy looking down, you don't see the knockout coming.

jokes.text: Well, I don't count the Capasso fight. That was a bad decision. / He knocked you out. / Oh, I mean the decision to fight him.
jokes.setup: Tony's boxing record and claim about a bad decision
jokes.punchline: Clarifying he means his decision, not the referee's
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect Tony self-awareness about his poor choices

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: The referee made a bad decision in that fight.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: What do you mean? The referee's decision was fine.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: No, I mean my decision to fight in the first place.

jokes.text: No, I mean how, you old bat.
jokes.setup: Aunt Lucia says Uncle Emilio died
jokes.punchline: Louie insults his elderly aunt while asking for details
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie rudeness, perfectly in character but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Your Uncle Emilio is dead? When did this happen?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Well, don't just stand there looking like a human raisin. Tell me what happened!

jokes.text: Well, we found him laying behind his favorite bench with a squirrel running back and forth across his face.
jokes.setup: Uncle Emilio's love of feeding animals in the park
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of his death scene
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd visual image that's both touching and funny

dialogue.speaker: Aunt Lucia
dialogue.line: Uncle Emilio, he went to the park to feed the pigeons like always. But this time, the pigeons, they were so hungry, so many of them. They surrounded him, and he couldn't get away. The pigeons, they ate him alive!

dialogue.speaker: Aunt Lucia
dialogue.line: When they found him, there was nothing left but his hat and his bag of breadcrumbs.

jokes.text: Well, uh, if he liked the park so much, why don't you just leave him where he is?
jokes.setup: Aunt Lucia asking for burial money
jokes.punchline: Louie's callous suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - practical and heartless solution

dialogue.speaker: Aunt Lucia
dialogue.line: Louie, I need to ask you something. When I die, I want a nice funeral. A real nice one.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Don't worry about it, Ma. I'll take care of everything.

dialogue.speaker: Aunt Lucia
dialogue.line: You promise? I want flowers, a nice casket, a good priest...

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Ma, forget about it. Here's what we'll do — when you die, we'll just throw you in the East River. Save everybody a lot of money.

jokes.text: Don't say no to me, Louie. I'm the only one in the family who hasn't put a curse on you.
jokes.setup: Louie's reluctance to give money
jokes.punchline: The threat involving family curses
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment and threat with supernatural elements

dialogue.speaker: Louie De Palma
dialogue.line: Family curses? What family curses?

dialogue.speaker: Aunt Lucia
dialogue.line: You don't give me the money, I'm gonna put a curse on you.

dialogue.speaker: Louie De Palma
dialogue.line: A curse? What kind of curse?

dialogue.speaker: Aunt Lucia
dialogue.line: The kind where you never make another dime the rest of your life.

dialogue.speaker: Louie De Palma
dialogue.line: That's not a curse, that's a prediction.

jokes.text: Thanks for the money, Louie!
jokes.setup: Tony getting knocked out in practice
jokes.punchline: Aunt Lucia celebrating Louie winning his bet
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing the bet is already paying off

dialogue.speaker: Aunt Lucia
dialogue.line: Tony, he's gonna be okay, right?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Of course he's gonna be okay. I got fifty bucks says he's out cold for ten minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Aunt Lucia
dialogue.line: You bet on my nephew getting knocked out?

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Yeah, and I won!

dialogue.speaker: Aunt Lucia
dialogue.line: Louie, that's beautiful!

jokes.text: Hey, Banta, you make up your mind yet? What's it gonna be, blindfold or cigarette?
jokes.setup: Pre-fight intimidation
jokes.punchline: Treating the fight like an execution
jokes.explanation: Dark humor comparing boxing to execution

dialogue.speaker: Louie De Palma
dialogue.line: You're gonna get destroyed out there. This ain't gonna be a fight, it's gonna be an execution.

dialogue.speaker: Tony Banta
dialogue.line: Yeah? Well, I've been executed before. I'm still here.

jokes.text: Don't worry, Alex. I think that punch took a lot out of him. / Yeah.
jokes.setup: Tony getting knocked down hard
jokes.punchline: Tony's delusional optimism
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor about Tony's delusional fight assessment

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Hey, no problem! I'm okay! I'm okay!

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: You know, that guy didn't mean it. He's probably just having a bad day.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Besides, I needed the exercise anyway.

jokes.text: The towel won't go. / Lucius! / Not you, him!
jokes.setup: Alex trying to throw in the towel
jokes.punchline: Lucius preventing him, then the confusion
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with good confusion about who should let go

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I'm done. I'm quitting.

dialogue.speaker: Lucius
dialogue.line: No, no, no! You can't quit!

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Why not? I've had it!

dialogue.speaker: Lucius
dialogue.line: Because you're the only one holding the towel!

jokes.text: What crowd? They hate me. They're here for him. I've never been cheered for in my life.
jokes.setup: Lucius asks if the crowd fires Tony up
jokes.punchline: Tony's depressing realization about his lack of fans
jokes.explanation: Touching and funny moment of self-awareness from Tony about his unpopularity

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Fire me up? The crowd doesn't even know I'm here. I could disappear tomorrow and nobody would notice.

jokes.text: For the duck! For the duck! For the duck!
jokes.setup: The inspirational duck story from earlier
jokes.punchline: The entire crowd chanting the absurd motivation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the duck callback - the absurd motivation becomes a rally cry

dialogue.speaker: Crowd
dialogue.line: You can do it! You can do it! You can do it!

jokes.text: For the duck, Tony. For the duck!
jokes.setup: Tony is losing the fight and needs motivation
jokes.punchline: Lucius brings back the absurd duck motivation at the crucial moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that elevates the absurd motivation to heroic status in the climactic moment

dialogue.speaker: Lucius
dialogue.line: You gotta think of the duck, Tony! Think of the duck!

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: The duck?!

dialogue.speaker: Lucius
dialogue.line: Yeah, man! That duck is countin' on you!

tmdb_overview: Tony begins working out with a new cabbie, Lucius, who is a former football player. Tony is in great shape all of a sudden and Lucius inspires Tony to try to get his boxing license reinstated. Tony is not hopeful that the boxing commission will reinstate him, so he is surprised when he gets his license back. In addition, he getes a major fight scheduled with a promising young fighter. Meanwhile, Lucius goes away to Miami to try out for the Dolphins. Without Lucius around, Tony loses his motivation and his training suffers. At the fight, he is getting pummelled until Lucius, who has learned that he has made the Dolphins, arrives to inspire him. With Lucius' support, Tony turns the fight around and knocks out his opponent.
insight: Tony's return generates 41 jokes in 41 minutes—character work carries an otherwise uneven comeback.
